meta.note: Small/skipped countries long-shot sweep. New countries added: Kyrgyzstan, Maldives, Malta, Cyprus (Oman & Sri Lanka already existed). Lady Chatterley's Lover is a Malta ban-add. Held: Nepal textbook (no retraceable author), Cambodia (no specific title). Most of Armenia/Georgia/Mongolia/Bhutan/Laos/Timor-Leste/Jamaica/Trinidad/Fiji/PNG/Luxembourg/Iceland yielded no retraceable title-specific ban.

entries.title: Examining the Scriptures Daily
entries.description_ban: Kyrgyzstan's State Commission for Religious Affairs denied import permission for the Kyrgyz-language 2022 edition of this Jehovah's Witness daily-text booklet; the refusal still stands.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific import refusal; documented by Forum 18.

entries.title: The Lessons for Little Ones
entries.description_ban: On 23 Feb 2023 the State Commission for Religious Affairs denied import permission for this Jehovah's Witness children's brochure in both Kyrgyz and Russian.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific import refusal; documented by Forum 18.

entries.title: Folk Tales of the Maldives
entries.description_ban: Ethnographic collection of Maldivian folk legends; banned by the strict-Islamic Maldivian government because the tales treat Islam casually rather than reverently.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific ban; documented via Wikipedia and an academic paper.

entries.title: I Am Waging a War with Words
entries.description_ban: Compilation of interviews by a Kilinochchi-based Tamil writer; seized by Sri Lanka Customs in March 2026 under Penal Code §120 and the Customs Ordinance as a threat to 'national harmony'.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific customs seizure; documented by Sri Lanka Brief and Tamil Guardian.

entries.title: Even Now There Are Two States
entries.description_ban: Collection of political columns by the same Tamil writer; detained in the same March 2026 Sri Lanka Customs consignment under §120 / Customs Ordinance for alleged threat to national security.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific customs seizure; documented by Sri Lanka Brief and WSWS.

entries.title: Cogwheel Buddha
entries.description_ban: Short-story collection (Däti Rōden Upan Budun) said to be the first banned book in Sri Lanka; it exposed the misconduct of religious, cultural and legal institutions toward the poor.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific ban; documented in a literary essay (medium confidence).

entries.title: Samahani
entries.description_ban: Novel set in 19th-century Zanzibar about an Omani princess and her castrated African slave; banned in Oman over its slavery and sexual content.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific ban; documented by CIVICUS Monitor and Words Without Borders.

entries.title: The Omani Spring
entries.description_ban: One of four Al-Hashimi titles seized at the 2018 Muscat International Book Fair, with no official reason given.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific book-fair seizure; documented by CIVICUS Monitor and GC4HR.

entries.title: The Gulf in the Time of Cholera
entries.description_ban: Seized at the 2018 Muscat International Book Fair among books by mostly Omani authors.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific book-fair seizure; documented by CIVICUS Monitor.

entries.title: Further than Zanzibar
entries.description_ban: On the Arabic Network for Human Rights Information's list of titles banned/seized at the 2010 Muscat book fair.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific book-fair seizure; documented via ArabLit (ANHRI list).

entries.title: The Prick
entries.description_ban: Omani novel (Al-Wakhz) on the 2010 ANHRI list of books banned/seized at the Muscat book fair.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific book-fair seizure; documented via ArabLit (ANHRI list).

entries.title: The Magic Toyshop
entries.description_ban: Among the ~43 titles from Anthony Burgess's personal library seized and destroyed by Malta's Postmaster General in 1968 under anti-obscenity/vilification-of-religion laws.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific confiscation/destruction; documented by the International Anthony Burgess Foundation.

entries.title: To the Devil a Daughter
entries.description_ban: Occult novel destroyed in the 1968 Malta confiscation of Anthony Burgess's library.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific confiscation/destruction; documented by the International Anthony Burgess Foundation.

entries.title: The Anti-Death League
entries.description_ban: Seized in the 1968 Malta sweep of Anthony Burgess's library but returned on appeal — a documented restriction rather than destruction.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific seizure (later returned); documented by the International Anthony Burgess Foundation.

entries.title: Lady Chatterley's Lover
entries.description_ban: Confiscated and destroyed from Anthony Burgess's library in Malta in 1968 under anti-obscenity laws. Adds a Malta ban to the existing record.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Adds a Malta ban; documented by the International Anthony Burgess Foundation.

entries.title: Spotlight on Britain
entries.description_ban: The Cyprus Ministry of Education pulled this OUP English-language school textbook over a page praising Atatürk as 'Turkey's greatest hero'; teachers were first told to tear out the page, then the book was dropped.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific ministry withdrawal; documented by Cyprus Mail and Euronews (medium confidence).
